etymology.description: Depicts a tool similar to a knife or a huge pair of scissors used to cut weed -> used phonetically for "to govern"
etymology.interpretation: The character 乂 originally depicted a tool for cutting, like a sickle or scissors, highlighting the action of control or management in agriculture. Over time, this imagery was metaphorically extended to mean 'govern' as effective governance is akin to managing and pruning society's needs.
